lines.text: Static from nowhere. The dungeon picked up a signal a long time ago and never let go.
Pages on the floor — declassified and then declassified again. The blacked-out sections contain more information than the visible text.
The bunker was built to survive something specific. The planners got the threat wrong. The bunker survived anyway — the wrong kind of monument to the wrong kind of fear.
A dispatch case lies open. Empty — the documents removed, the clasps still bright. Whoever emptied it was in a hurry. Whoever they were reporting to never received the report.
The twentieth century left more here than any other. Too much was recorded, classified, lost, destroyed, and preserved in the wrong order.
An old frequency plays at the edge of hearing — news, propaganda, something between them. The broadcast still cycling. Nobody left to receive it but the walls.
Intelligence archives do not reveal their organization to the uninitiated. The filing system here belongs to a bureaucracy that no longer exists. The files remain.
A code word is written on the wall. It was classified once. It has been declassified. It was reclassified. The classification levels are listed beneath it — the list is longer than the word.
The costs of the century are itemized somewhere in the lower levels. The dungeon does not post summaries. It keeps the full accounting.
The last operator of this level left their post voluntarily, involuntarily, or by means the record describes only as 'operational conclusion.' The post is yours now.
